title: City of Bone
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Structure

Built as an [[amusement park]] on the outskirts of [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore's]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], the '''City of Bone''' would become home to an Imperial [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] following the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Declaration of a New Order|rise]] to power and its occupation of Mandalore. It also became the central hub of the Empire's [[Slavery/Legends|slave]] and [[Mining/Legends|mining]] operations, until finally being [[Battle of the City of Bone|destroyed]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]].

==History==
===Amusement park===
{{Quote|It was the dumbest idea. Hayar's idiot brother thought Mandalore could attract adventure tourists.|[[Jarkyc]]|<ref name="ImpCom501st" />}}
Convinced that there was an untapped potential for tourism on [[Mandalore/Legends|Mandalore]], an [[Hayar's brother|enterprising Mandalorian]] constructed an oversized replica of a [[Mythosaur/Legends|mythosaur]] [[Skeleton/Legends|skeleton]] on the outskirts of [[Keldabe/Legends|Keldabe]], intending to build an [[amusement park]] in and around it. However, the idea was doomed to fail and the park never opened.<ref name="ImpCom501st" />

===Imperial stronghold===
{{Quote|We've been bad boys. We told them it was an ancient Mandalorian temple that held great magical power for us simple folk, and so...well, they wanted to build the garrison in there, on account of it having so much ''significance'' to us. So we ''sold it to them''.|[[Jarkyc]]|<ref name="ImpCom501st" />}}
When the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] arrived on Mandalore to construct a base shortly after the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] [[Hayar]] and [[Jarkyc]] convinced the Imperial [[Unidentified Imperial commander (Mandalore)|garrison commander]] that the structure was an ancient Mandalorian [[temple]] of great spiritual importance to the people. The commander bought the false "temple" with the belief that an occupation of the structure would have some adverse effect on the Mandalorian population, and established the [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] there. [[Dred Priest]], a former member of the ''[[Cuy'val Dar]]'' in service to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic's]] [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Grand Army]] and now a member of the radical [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] group, assisted the Imperials establish the garrison in the park.<ref name="ImpCom501st" />

The structure remained under Imperial control long into the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], and it was from the City of Bone that the [[Suprema]] supervised his [[Slavery/Legends|slaver]] operation. It was [[Battle of the City of Bone|destroyed]] in [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]] by [[Mandalorian Supercommando]] [[Tobbi Dala]] by closing the hangar that based [[Shrike Squadron (Mandalore)|Shrike Squadron]], creating an explosive chain reaction that took his own life in the process.<ref name="SW69" />

==Interior==
[[File:City_of_Bone.jpg|thumb|right|150px|Inside the City of Bone.]]
Inside the city there were detention centers for slaves and a hangar for air deployment and slave shipment. The slaves were treated and handled like animals, and fed poorly.
